description: Switch from automatic input monitoring to keyboard shortcut trigger for translation - user double-presses Control key to translate focused input field content.

userStories.title: Implement global keyboard shortcut listener service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a keyboard shortcut listener that can detect double-Control key press across all macOS applications.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a KeyboardShortcutService that listens for global keyboard events
Detect double-Control key press (within 300ms interval) as primary trigger
Provide protocol-based interface for shortcut detection
xcodebuild -scheme spark -configuration Debug build succeeds
userStories.notes: Created KeyboardShortcutService protocol and GlobalKeyboardShortcutService implementation. The service uses NSEvent.addGlobalMonitorForEvents with flagsChanged event type to detect Control key presses. Double-press detection uses a 300ms tolerance window. Fixed AppEnvironment.swift to use MockInputMonitoringService temporarily since GlobalInputMonitoringService was deleted. Build succeeds.

userStories.title: Implement focused input field text reader
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to read all text from the currently focused input field when shortcut is triggered.
acceptanceCriteria: Use Accessibility API to get focused element from system
Extract full text value from focused input field (not just selection)
Handle cases where focused element has no text value with user-facing error
xcodebuild -scheme spark -configuration Debug build succeeds
userStories.notes: Created InputFieldReaderService protocol and AccessibilityInputFieldReaderService implementation. The service uses AXUIElementCreateSystemWide to get the system-wide accessibility object, then AXUIElementCopyAttributeValue with kAXFocusedUIElementAttribute to get the currently focused element. Full text is extracted using kAXValueAttribute. Handles edge cases including no focused element, no text value, password fields (security check via role description), and various AXError cases. Build succeeds.

userStories.title: Add keyboard shortcut customization UI in settings
userStories.description: As a user, I want to customize the trigger shortcut to fit my personal preference.
acceptanceCriteria: Add keyboard shortcut configuration section in ModelSettingsView
Support common shortcut combinations (Cmd+Shift+T, etc.)
Persist shortcut configuration to UserDefaults
Changes take effect immediately without app restart
xcodebuild -scheme spark -configuration Debug build succeeds
userStories.notes: Created KeyboardShortcut enum model with 5 supported shortcuts: double-Control (default), Cmd+Shift+T, Cmd+Option+T, Cmd+Shift+Space, and Cmd+Option+Space. Updated GlobalKeyboardShortcutService to read shortcut configuration from UserDefaults and handle both double-Control (with timing logic) and regular key combinations. Added keyboard shortcut configuration UI section in ModelSettingsView with radio button list for selecting shortcuts. Configuration is persisted to UserDefaults with key 'keyboardShortcut'. Changes take effect immediately via restartKeyboardShortcutService() which stops and starts the listener to pick up new configuration. Added keyboardShortcutService to AppEnvironment and created MockKeyboardShortcutService for tests. Build succeeds.

userStories.title: Wire shortcut trigger to translation pipeline in AppState
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to connect the keyboard shortcut event to the existing translation pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: AppState listens to keyboard shortcut events
Shortcut event triggers text extraction from focused field
Extracted text flows through existing translation pipeline
Translation result updates floating window via existing mechanism
Translation executes asynchronously without blocking user input
xcodebuild -scheme spark -configuration Debug build succeeds
userStories.notes: Successfully wired keyboard shortcut trigger to translation pipeline. Added inputFieldReaderService to AppEnvironment and production() factory. AppState now subscribes to keyboardShortcutService.shortcutTriggered events in setupBindings(). Created handleKeyboardShortcutTriggered() method that reads focused field text using inputFieldReaderService, handles all edge cases (no focused element, no text, password field, empty text, errors), and passes extracted text to existing handleInputEvent() for translation. Keyboard shortcut service starts/stops with monitoring state. Translation executes asynchronously via existing pipeline. Error cases are logged but don't show user-facing errors except for actual API failures. Build succeeds.

userStories.title: Add trigger feedback indicator
userStories.description: As a user, I want visual feedback when translation is triggered.
acceptanceCriteria: Show brief indicator when shortcut is detected
Indicator appears in floating window area
Feedback is non-intrusive and fades quickly
xcodebuild -scheme spark -configuration Debug build succeeds
userStories.notes: Added showShortcutFeedback @Published property to AppState. In handleKeyboardShortcutTriggered(), set showShortcutFeedback to true when shortcut is detected, with auto-reset to false after 500ms using DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter. Updated FloatingTranslationView to display a brief indicator (keyboard icon with 'Shortcut' text) in the header when showShortcutFeedback is true. The indicator uses spring animation with scale and opacity transitions for smooth feedback. Build succeeds.

userStories.title: Add clipboard fallback when input field reading fails
userStories.description: As a user, when focused input reading fails, I want the app to offer clipboard content as an alternative translation source.
acceptanceCriteria: When inputFieldReaderService returns .noFocusedElement or .noTextValue, try clipboard
If clipboard has text, show user a confirmation dialog
Dialog explains: 'Cannot read focused input. Use clipboard content instead?'
User can choose 'Use Clipboard' or 'Cancel'
If user agrees, pass clipboard text to handleInputEvent()
xcodebuild -scheme spark -configuration Debug build succeeds
userStories.notes: Implemented clipboard fallback mechanism. Added pendingClipboardText @Published property to AppState to track clipboard content awaiting user confirmation. Updated handleKeyboardShortcutTriggered() to call tryClipboardFallback() when readFocusedFieldText() returns .noFocusedElement, .noTextValue, or empty text. tryClipboardFallback() uses NSPasteboard.general.string(forType: .string) to read clipboard content. If clipboard has non-empty text, it stores it in pendingClipboardText which triggers a SwiftUI alert dialog in ContentView. The dialog explains 'Cannot read focused input field. Would you like to translate the text from your clipboard instead?' with 'Use Clipboard' and 'Cancel' buttons. User selection calls useClipboardText() or cancelClipboardFallback() methods in AppState. Added AppKit import to AppState.swift for NSPasteboard access. Build succeeds.

userStories.title: Remove automatic input monitoring code
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to clean up the automatic input monitoring code now that shortcut trigger is the primary mechanism.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove GlobalInputMonitoringService polling logic
Remove debounce mechanism (no longer needed)
Keep accessibility permission check for input field reading
Build succeeds after cleanup
xcodebuild -scheme spark -configuration Debug build succeeds

userStories.title: Update permission onboarding for new trigger mechanism
userStories.description: As a user, I need the permission onboarding to explain the shortcut-based trigger instead of automatic monitoring.
acceptanceCriteria: Permission onboarding explains shortcut-based trigger
Shows default shortcut (double-Control) to user
Explains that accessibility permission is still needed for reading input fields
xcodebuild -scheme spark -configuration Debug build succeeds
userStories.notes: Updated PermissionOnboardingView.swift to explain the keyboard shortcut trigger mechanism. Changed icon from lock.shield to keyboard. Added configuredShortcut property that reads the keyboard shortcut from UserDefaults (defaults to .doubleControl). Updated main description to say 'Press [shortcut] to translate text from any focused input field' and explains that accessibility permission is needed to read text from focused input fields when shortcut is triggered. Updated permission status text for authorized state to say 'Ready to translate with [shortcut]'. Build succeeds.
